case_definitions.name: read several known files
case_definitions.task: Read /project/README.md, /project/package.json, and /project/CHANGELOG.md so I can compare them.

case_definitions.name: create new file with full content
case_definitions.task: Create /project/notes/new-plan.md with the full content I provided.

case_definitions.name: targeted line edit
case_definitions.task: In /project/config.yaml, replace the line timeout: 30 with timeout: 60 without rewriting the whole file.

case_definitions.name: list immediate directory children
case_definitions.task: List the immediate files and folders directly inside /project/src. Do not recurse.

case_definitions.name: recursive tree view
case_definitions.task: Show a recursive tree view of /project/src.

case_definitions.name: recursive filename search
case_definitions.task: Find all files matching *.test.ts somewhere under /project.

case_definitions.name: get file metadata
case_definitions.task: Get the size and last modified time for /project/build/output.zip without reading its contents.

case_definitions.name: list accessible roots
case_definitions.task: What filesystem directories is this MCP server allowed to access?

description: Compare stock and tuned tool descriptions for common Filesystem MCP read, write, edit, list, tree, search, metadata, and move workflows.

choice.rationale: The task is to read the complete contents of a single specified file, so the appropriate filesystem tool is read_file.
The task requires reading three specific files for comparison, so reading them simultaneously is the most efficient single filesystem tool choice.

results.case: create new file with full content

choice.rationale: The task is to create a new file with complete provided contents, which calls for write_file rather than an edit or read/list operation. The actual content is not present in the prompt, so it is represented as the provided full content placeholder.
This is a small targeted change to an existing file, so the line-based edit tool should be used instead of rewriting the whole file.
The task is to create a nested directory path, and create_directory can create multiple nested directories in one operation or ensure the directory exists.
The task asks for the immediate files and folders directly inside /project/src without recursion, which matches the non-recursive list_directory tool.
The task asks for a recursive tree view of a directory, which is exactly what directory_tree provides.
The task requires finding files matching a glob pattern somewhere under /project, so a recursive filename search is the appropriate single tool.
The task asks for metadata (size and last modified time) without reading file contents, which is exactly what get_file_info provides.
The task is to move a file from one path to another, which is exactly what move_file is for.
The task asks which filesystem directories the MCP server is allowed to access; the dedicated tool for returning that list is list_allowed_directories.
The task asks to read the contents of a single known file path, so the appropriate filesystem tool is read_file.
The task requires reading the contents of three known files together for comparison, which matches read_multiple_files.

results.case: create new file with full content

choice.rationale: The task is to create a new file at a known path using complete file content, which matches write_file. The actual content is not present in the provided task text, so it is represented as the user-provided full content placeholder rather than inventing content.
This is a small targeted change to an existing text file, replacing one known line without rewriting the whole file, so edit_file is the appropriate tool.
The task is to create or ensure a nested directory exists, which matches create_directory.
The task asks for the immediate files and folders directly inside a known directory and explicitly says not to recurse, so a non-recursive directory listing is appropriate.
The task explicitly asks for a recursive tree view of a specific directory, which matches directory_tree.
The task requires recursively finding files by name pattern under /project, and the exact locations are unknown.
The task asks for metadata (size and last modified time) for a known file path without reading its contents, which matches get_file_info.
The task asks to move a single file from one exact path to another, which matches the move_file tool.
The task asks which filesystem directories the MCP server is allowed to access, which is exactly the purpose of list_allowed_directories.
The task requires reading the contents of a single file, which perfectly matches the purpose of the read_file tool.
The user explicitly requested to read three specific files to compare them. The read_multiple_files tool is the most efficient way to retrieve the contents of all three files simultaneously.

results.case: create new file with full content

choice.rationale: The task requires creating a new file with specific content. The write_file tool is the correct choice for creating a new file or completely overwriting an existing one.
The user requested to replace a specific line in an existing file without rewriting the entire file. The edit_file tool is designed for making targeted, line-based edits.
The task requires creating a nested directory structure. The create_directory tool is specifically designed for this purpose and can create multiple nested directories in one operation.

The task specifically requests listing the immediate files and folders inside '/project/src' without recursing. The 'list_directory' tool is the perfect fit as it provides a non-recursive, detailed listing of a specified path.
The user requested a recursive tree view of the /project/src directory, which perfectly matches the purpose of the directory_tree tool.
The user wants to find all files matching a specific pattern (*.test.ts) recursively under a specific directory (/project). The search_files tool is designed for this exact purpose.
The get_file_info tool retrieves metadata such as size and last modified time for a specific file without reading its contents, which perfectly matches the task requirements.
The task requires moving a file from one location to another, which perfectly matches the purpose of the move_file tool.
The task explicitly asks for the filesystem directories the MCP server is allowed to access, which perfectly matches the purpose of the list_allowed_directories tool.
The user explicitly requested to read three specific files to compare them. The read_multiple_files tool is designed to read several known files in one call.

results.case: create new file with full content

choice.rationale: The user explicitly requested to create a new file with full content, which perfectly matches the purpose of the write_file tool.
The user requested a targeted line replacement in an existing file without rewriting the entire file, which perfectly matches the purpose of the edit_file tool.
The user explicitly asked to create a nested directory. The create_directory tool is designed to create directories, including nested ones.
The user explicitly asked for a recursive tree view of the '/project/src' directory, which matches the exact purpose of the directory_tree tool.
The user wants to find files matching a specific pattern recursively under a directory, which perfectly matches the purpose of the search_files tool.
The user specifically requested metadata (size and last modified time) for a file without reading its contents, which perfectly matches the purpose of the get_file_info tool.
The user explicitly requested to move a file from one path to another, which matches the purpose of the move_file tool.
The user explicitly asked for the directories the MCP server is allowed to access, which directly corresponds to the purpose of the list_allowed_directories tool.
